clusters.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia must join Abraham Accords to seal nuclear energy deal
clusters.neutral_headline: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
bullet_summary: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed
President Trump said Thursday on social media that Saudi Arabia must agree to join the Abraham Accords as a contingency on a U
After a Saudi nuclear power deal raised alarm in Israel, President Trump announced online that the arrangement is subject to the kingdom normalizing relations with Israel through the Abraham Accords

items.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia must join Abraham Accords to seal nuclear energy deal
items.standfirst: President Trump said Thursday on Truth Social that Saudi Arabia must normalize diplomatic relations with Israel by joining the Abraham Accords as a contingency to the enactment of the nuclear agreement that was signed on Wednesday. Natalie Brand shares the details.
items.content: President Trump said Thursday on Truth Social that Saudi Arabia must normalize diplomatic relations with Israel by joining the Abraham Accords as a contingency to the enactment of the nuclear agreement that was signed on Wednesday. Natalie Brand shares the details.

items.title: Trump stuns Saudis by pinning nuclear deal to Israel ties
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed. Riyadh has long resisted joining the so-called Abraham Accords with Israel, insisting especially after the Gaza conflict on a pathway to a Palestinian state. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia depends on the kingdom recognizing Israel -- throwing the agreement into turmoil just the day after it was signed. Riyadh has long resisted joining the so-called Abraham Accords with Israel, insisting especially after the Gaza conflict on a pathway to a Palestinian state. FRANCE 24's Emerald Maxwell reports.

items.title: Saudis must recognise Israel for nuclear deal, says Trump
items.standfirst: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.
items.content: The US president says the landmark agreement to help Saudis develop nuclear energy is dependent on it joining the Abraham Accords.

items.title: Trump: US-Saudi nuclear deal hinges on Saudi joining Abraham Accords
items.standfirst: The US and Saudi Arabia have reached a civilian nuclear cooperation deal.
items.content: The US and Saudi Arabia have reached a civilian nuclear cooperation deal.

items.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia must join Abraham Accords as contingency of nuclear deal
items.standfirst: President Trump said Thursday on social media that Saudi Arabia must agree to join the Abraham Accords as a contingency on a U.S.-Saudi Arabia nuclear agreement. In a separate post, he threatened Iran and its Houthi allies with "major military punishment." CBS News' Kathryn Watson has more.
items.content: President Trump said Thursday on social media that Saudi Arabia must agree to join the Abraham Accords as a contingency on a U.S.-Saudi Arabia nuclear agreement. In a separate post, he threatened Iran and its Houthi allies with "major military punishment." CBS News' Kathryn Watson has more.

items.title: Trump says Saudi nuclear pact hinges on kingdom joining Abraham Accords
items.standfirst: Claim casts further confusion over deal to launch US-supported civilian nuclear programme in Saudi Arabia.
items.content: Claim casts further confusion over deal to launch US-supported civilian nuclear programme in Saudi Arabia.

items.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia must normalize Israel ties before nuclear deal is approved
items.standfirst: President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal between the United States and Saudi Arabia requires the kingdom to normalize relations with Israel — a significant condition not previously mentioned as the deal was announced.
items.content: President Donald Trump said Thursday that a nuclear deal between the United States and Saudi Arabia requires the kingdom to normalize relations with Israel — a significant condition not previously mentioned as the deal was announced.

items.title: U.S. signs nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia. And, Nolan Wells' independent autopsy results
items.standfirst: The U.S. has signed a nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia. And, the independent autopsy results of Nolan Wells fuel more questions surrounding his cause of death.
items.content: The U.S. has signed a nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia. And, the independent autopsy results of Nolan Wells fuel more questions surrounding his cause of death.(Image credit: Brendan Smialowski)

items.title: Trump says Saudi Arabia's nuclear deal hinges on the kingdom normalizing Israel ties
items.standfirst: After a Saudi nuclear power deal raised alarm in Israel, President Trump announced online that the arrangement is subject to the kingdom normalizing relations with Israel through the Abraham Accords.
items.content: After a Saudi nuclear power deal raised alarm in Israel, President Trump announced online that the arrangement is subject to the kingdom normalizing relations with Israel through the Abraham Accords.(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)

items.title: US signs nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
items.standfirst: The US and Saudi Arabia have reached a nuclear cooperation deal.
items.content: The US and Saudi Arabia have reached a nuclear cooperation deal.

items.title: U.S. signs landmark nuclear agreement with Saudi Arabia
items.standfirst: The U.S. will support creating a civilian nuclear power program in the kingdom under the agreement, a senior administration official told NBC News.
items.content: The U.S. will support creating a civilian nuclear power program in the kingdom under the agreement, a senior administration official told NBC News.

clusters.title: Oil hits $100 for first time since May while US stocks slide
clusters.neutral_headline: Oil surges past $100 in a first since May as Middle East conflicts rage
bullet_summary: Reported by Financial Times, Al Jazeera, BBC World and others

items.title: Oil hits $100 for first time since May while US stocks slide
items.standfirst: Donald Trump warns US is weighing ‘massive attack’ on Iran as Middle East tensions ratchet higher
items.content: Donald Trump warns US is weighing ‘massive attack’ on Iran as Middle East tensions ratchet higher

items.title: Oil surges past $100 in a first since May as Middle East conflicts rage
items.standfirst: Oil prices rise for a fifth day after Yemen's Houthis say they struck Saudi Arabian oil tankers in the Red Sea.
items.content: Oil prices rise for a fifth day after Yemen's Houthis say they struck Saudi Arabian oil tankers in the Red Sea.

items.title: Oil prices hit $100 for the first time since May
items.standfirst: The price of Brent crude rose more than 6% on Thursday as the war in the Middle East continues to escalate.
items.content: The price of Brent crude rose more than 6% on Thursday as the war in the Middle East continues to escalate.

items.title: Middle East war's economic impact 'could get worse' if Houthis join in
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump threatened Yemen's Houthi rebels with "major military punishment" after they launched missile and drone strikes on oil tankers in the Red Sea. Concern has sparked across the globe regarding the rebels' involvement in the war. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.
items.content: US President Donald Trump threatened Yemen's Houthi rebels with "major military punishment" after they launched missile and drone strikes on oil tankers in the Red Sea. Concern has sparked across the globe regarding the rebels' involvement in the war. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.

items.title: US rhetoric 'all about amping up' but strategy for a deal with Iran unclear
items.standfirst: After a twelfth night of US strikes, Iran has hit US bases in Jordan and Kuwait, claiming several military assets were destroyed. This also comes after rebel Houthis in Yemen struck two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea, further expanding the conflict. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.
items.content: After a twelfth night of US strikes, Iran has hit US bases in Jordan and Kuwait, claiming several military assets were destroyed. This also comes after rebel Houthis in Yemen struck two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea, further expanding the conflict. FRANCE 24's Kethevane Gorjestani tells us more.

items.title: How Houthi strikes on oil tankers will impact the Middle East war
items.standfirst: Yemen's Houthi rebels say they have attacked two Saudi oil tankers. The Houthis are supported by Iran, and the attacks in the Red Sea could be a new front for the war between the US and Iran. But how will this affect the conflict?
items.content: Yemen's Houthi rebels say they have attacked two Saudi oil tankers. The Houthis are supported by Iran, and the attacks in the Red Sea could be a new front for the war between the US and Iran. But how will this affect the conflict?

clusters.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
clusters.neutral_headline: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, France24, Sky News World

items.title: France evacuates 20,000 as wildfires spread in south-west
items.standfirst: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.
items.content: Most of those evacuated were tourists from campsites around Arcachon Bay in France's Gironde region.

items.title: France : Wildfire sweeps through 2,000 hectares near tourist hotspot
items.standfirst: A wildfire is ripping through pine forests in the southwest of France, near the tourist hotspot of Arcachon. Nearly 12,000 people, including tourists staying at campgrounds, have been evacuated.
items.content: A wildfire is ripping through pine forests in the southwest of France, near the tourist hotspot of Arcachon. Nearly 12,000 people, including tourists staying at campgrounds, have been evacuated.

items.title: In pictures: Wildfires ravage parts of southern France
items.standfirst: Thousands of people were evacuated in southwest France on Thursday, as a wildfire ravaged a seaside pine forest. Hundreds of firefighters were battling the fire, which started around midday on Wednesday. FRANCE 24 looks at some of the most striking images of the fire.
items.content: Thousands of people were evacuated in southwest France on Thursday, as a wildfire ravaged a seaside pine forest. Hundreds of firefighters were battling the fire, which started around midday on Wednesday. FRANCE 24 looks at some of the most striking images of the fire.

items.title: More than 15,000 people evacuated from homes as wildfire spreads in France
items.standfirst: Around 20,000 people were evacuated from towns and holiday resorts in southwestern France as a wildfire raged near the popular tourist coastal town of Lege Cap-Ferret.
items.content: Around 20,000 people were evacuated from towns and holiday resorts in southwestern France as a wildfire raged near the popular tourist coastal town of Lege Cap-Ferret.

items.content: The Justice Department will face an uphill battle if it tries to prosecute Jack Smith over allegations leveled by House Republicans, former prosecutors say.

items.title: Republicans urge Justice Department to investigate former prosecutor Jack Smith
items.standfirst: The former special counsel led two criminal investigations of President Trump and has been a frequent target of Trump's ire on social media.

clusters.title: Ukraine strikes warehouses of top Russian online retailer Wildberries, harms logistics capacity

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, BBC World

items.title: Ukraine strikes warehouses of top Russian online retailer Wildberries, harms logistics capacity
items.standfirst: Since the weekend, Ukraine has struck four warehouses belonging to leading Russian retailer Wildberries, often described as the country's Amazon. Aidan Stretch tells the story from Kyiv.
items.content: Since the weekend, Ukraine has struck four warehouses belonging to leading Russian retailer Wildberries, often described as the country's Amazon. Aidan Stretch tells the story from Kyiv.

items.title: Russia's businesses under strain from Ukraine's attacks on Wildberries
items.standfirst: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.
items.content: Ukrainian drones have hit several retail warehouses belonging to Russia's biggest online retailer Wildberries in a matter of days.

clusters.title: Reinstating Fedorov 'only right decision Zelensky can make', MP says
clusters.neutral_headline: Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated

items.title: Reinstating Fedorov 'only right decision Zelensky can make', MP says
items.standfirst: Ousted Ukrainian defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov said on Thursday he would ​accept no other government position besides his former job, escalating a political crisis that has gripped Ukraine at a pivotal point in the war with Russia. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Ukranian MP Liudmyla Buimister says that reinstating Fedorov "is the only right decision that President Zelensky can make".
items.content: Ousted Ukrainian defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov said on Thursday he would ​accept no other government position besides his former job, escalating a political crisis that has gripped Ukraine at a pivotal point in the war with Russia. Speaking with FRANCE 24's Mark Owen, Ukranian MP Liudmyla Buimister says that reinstating Fedorov "is the only right decision that President Zelensky can make".

items.title: Ukraine's ousted defence minister insists on being reinstated
items.standfirst: Mykhailo Fedorov refuses President Zelensky's offer to accept an alternative post, as the battle of wills continues.
items.content: Mykhailo Fedorov refuses President Zelensky's offer to accept an alternative post, as the battle of wills continues.

clusters.title: Vance Boelter gets 2 life terms and more in Minnesota lawmaker shootings
clusters.neutral_headline: Minnesota man sentenced to life for killing Democratic lawmaker Melissa Hortman
bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Guardian World

items.title: Vance Boelter gets 2 life terms and more in Minnesota lawmaker shootings
items.standfirst: Vance Boelter, the man who admitted in court to shooting two Minnesota lawmakers and their spouses, was sentenced to two life terms in prison followed by 40 years Thursday morning.
items.content: Vance Boelter, the man who admitted in court to shooting two Minnesota lawmakers and their spouses, was sentenced to two life terms in prison followed by 40 years Thursday morning.

items.title: Minnesota man sentenced to life for killing Democratic lawmaker Melissa Hortman
items.standfirst: Vance Boelter receives two life terms after admitting politically motivated killings and shootingsSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe Minnesota man who pleaded guilty to killing a top Democratic lawmaker and her husband was sentenced to spend the rest of his life in prison on Thursday, a little over a year after the political attacks.Vance Boelter, 59, was sentenced to two consecutive life terms plus 40 years in prison after pleading guilty in June to the six counts related to the killings of the former house speaker Melissa Hortman and her husband, Mark, and the shootings of state senator John Hoffman and his wife, Yvette. Continue reading...
items.content: Vance Boelter receives two life terms after admitting politically motivated killings and shootingsSign up for the Breaking News US newsletter emailThe Minnesota man who pleaded guilty to killing a top Democratic lawmaker and her husband was sentenced to spend the rest of his life in prison on Thursday, a little over a year after the political attacks.Vance Boelter, 59, was sentenced to two consecutive life terms plus 40 years in prison after pleading guilty in June to the six counts related to the killings of the former house speaker Melissa Hortman and her husband, Mark, and the shootings of state senator John Hoffman and his wife, Yvette. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Secret Service agent on Vance’s detail under investigation
clusters.neutral_headline: Secret Service agent on Vance’s detail under investigation
bullet_summary: The Secret Service said a member of Vice President JD Vance's detail is under investigation for "compromising operational and information security
Reported by Guardian World, CBS News

items.title: Secret Service agent on Vance’s detail under investigation
items.standfirst: Agent came under suspicion after allegedly providing info in MS Now report about vice-president’s helicopter tripA Secret Service agent assigned to JD Vance’s protective detail is “the subject of an administrative investigation, and potential criminal inquiry, involving allegations of compromising operational and information security”, according to the Secret Service chief of communications.The agent came under suspicion after allegedly providing information that appeared in an MS Now report about the vice-president’s planned Marine Corps helicopter trip with his son to a golf lesson, a source told CNN. Continue reading...
items.content: Agent came under suspicion after allegedly providing info in MS Now report about vice-president’s helicopter tripA Secret Service agent assigned to JD Vance’s protective detail is “the subject of an administrative investigation, and potential criminal inquiry, involving allegations of compromising operational and information security”, according to the Secret Service chief of communications.The agent came under suspicion after allegedly providing information that appeared in an MS Now report about the vice-president’s planned Marine Corps helicopter trip with his son to a golf lesson, a source told CNN. Continue reading...

clusters.title: For a 2nd time, House approves resolution to end the war in Iran in a rebuke to Trump

bullet_summary: Reported by NPR News, CBS News

items.title: For a 2nd time, House approves resolution to end the war in Iran in a rebuke to Trump
items.standfirst: Four Republicans joined Democrats to approve the measure. And while it is not legally binding, the resolution represents growing frustration inside Congress over the war.
items.content: Four Republicans joined Democrats to approve the measure. And while it is not legally binding, the resolution represents growing frustration inside Congress over the war. (Image credit: J. Scott Applewhite)

items.title: House again rebukes Trump over Iran, while similar effort stalls in Senate
items.standfirst: The House on Thursday voted to direct President Trump to end the war with Iran, after four Republicans joined Democrats to rebuke the president over his handling of the conflict.
items.content: The House on Thursday voted to direct President Trump to end the war with Iran, after four Republicans joined Democrats to rebuke the president over his handling of the conflict.

clusters.title: Senate fails to advance latest attempt to limit Trump's Iran war powers

bullet_summary: Reported by CBS News, Al Jazeera

items.title: Senate fails to advance latest attempt to limit Trump's Iran war powers
items.standfirst: The Senate rejected an attempt to rein in President Trump's war powers in Iran, soon after the House adopted a separate measure rebuking the president amid escalations in the conflict in recent days.
items.content: The Senate rejected an attempt to rein in President Trump's war powers in Iran, soon after the House adopted a separate measure rebuking the president amid escalations in the conflict in recent days.

items.title: US House votes to limit Iran war for first time since ceasefire breakdown
items.standfirst: Senate fails to pass latest War Powers resolution, in largely symbolic move attempting to reign in Trump.
items.content: Senate fails to pass latest War Powers resolution, in largely symbolic move attempting to reign in Trump.

clusters.title: Why health officials do not have a good handle on the current Ebola outbreak
clusters.neutral_headline: Why health officials do not have a good handle on the current Ebola outbreak
bullet_summary: More than 1,000 people have died in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), health officials said
Reported by NPR News, Sky News World

items.title: Why health officials do not have a good handle on the current Ebola outbreak
items.standfirst: To get slightly ahead of the virus, health officials need to quickly identify people at risk of infection, a practice known as contact tracing. That's a challenge in the Democratic Republic of Congo.
items.content: To get slightly ahead of the virus, health officials need to quickly identify people at risk of infection, a practice known as contact tracing. That's a challenge in the Democratic Republic of Congo.(Image credit: Michel Lunanga)

items.title: More than 1,000 people killed in Ebola outbreak
items.standfirst: More than 1,000 people have died in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), health officials said.
items.content: More than 1,000 people have died in the Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), health officials said.
